summary:
Goblet cells spread evenly throughout the villi but do not move up specifically; whereas Paneth cells cluster at the core/vectical tip of the villi showing clear upward migration during their lifecycle stages before specialization into mature forms elsewhere within gut tissues. \boxed{D}
To summarize Agent1’s reasoning concisely:

The stem/progenitor cells migrate upward from the intestinal crypts toward the tips of the villi where different specialized cells like Paneth gather more densely, indicating active proliferation moving 'upwards'. This contrasts with uniform distribution observed among non-proliferating cell types such as goblets. Thus,
\[ D \] is correct based on cellular behavior described here. 

This summary captures both essential aspects—migration pattern and specific location relevance—to address the original query effectively.
